skill.name: hypothesis-generation
skill.description: Generate evidence-based scientific hypotheses from observations, literature, and preliminary data. The skill structures competing mechanisms, testable predictions, experiment designs, and LaTeX report output.
skill.summary: Generate testable scientific hypotheses with predictions, experiments, and structured reports.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns. Manual review found no executable source code, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection. Most alerts are Markdown code examples, LaTeX syntax, scientific terminology, or public PubMed search examples.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands Require User Execution
low_findings.description: The skill includes Bash and LaTeX command examples for generating schematics and compiling reports. They are documentation snippets, not hidden code, but users should review commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are clearly inside Markdown code fences and relate to the stated reporting workflow. Risk remains low because the skill declares Bash use and the commands are not automatically executed.

low_findings.title: Public Literature Search URLs
low_findings.description: The network findings are PubMed URL examples used to teach literature search strategy. They do not include secrets, tracking endpoints, or instructions to send private data to a third party.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to PubMed search pages and use generic example terms or placeholders. No evidence found of credential collection, covert networking, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high static alerts for weak cryptography occur in scientific and LaTeX text, not cryptographic code. Examples include hypothesis descriptions, design guidance, and formatting references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, authentication, or cryptographic API usage. The flagged words are semantic false positives from domain vocabulary and document formatting.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged ordinary research-methodology text as reconnaissance. Manual review found no host enumeration, environment probing, or system information collection instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss language, controls, assumptions, and page breaks in research documents. No evidence found of commands or logic that inspect the user system.

content.value_statement: Researchers often have observations but need clear, testable explanations. This skill turns evidence into competing hypotheses, predictions, experiments, and structured reports.
actual_capabilities: Turns observations or preliminary data into 3-5 competing scientific hypotheses.
Connects hypotheses to literature search strategies and evidence synthesis.
Evaluates hypotheses using testability, falsifiability, parsimony, and explanatory power.
Designs experiments with controls, measurements, confounds, and statistical considerations.
Produces a structured LaTeX hypothesis report with main text and appendices.
Guides use of schematic figures for mechanisms, workflows, and decision trees.
limitations: It does not verify scientific truth without current literature review and expert judgment.
It may require web search tools for recent papers and citations.
LaTeX compilation requires local tools such as XeLaTeX or LuaLaTeX.
Generated experiments still need ethical, regulatory, and feasibility review.

use_cases.title: Frame a New Research Question
use_cases.description: Convert an unexplained observation into competing mechanisms and measurable predictions.

use_cases.title: Plan Early Experiments
use_cases.description: Design first-pass experiments with controls, outcomes, and discriminating tests.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Research Brief
use_cases.description: Create a concise hypothesis report with appendices for evidence and methods.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Starter Hypotheses
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for an initial observation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hypothesis-generation on this observation: [describe observation]. Give 3 testable hypotheses and one prediction for each.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Mechanisms
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hypothesis-generation to explain [phenomenon]. Compare 4 mechanisms, key evidence, assumptions, and falsifying observations.

prompt_templates.title: Design Experiments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hypothesis-generation for [hypothesis set]. Design experiments with controls, measurements, confounds, and expected outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a structured deliverable.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use hypothesis-generation to create a LaTeX report for [phenomenon]. Include hypotheses, predictions, comparisons, and appendix plans.

output_examples.input: Observation: treated cells recover faster after stress exposure.
output: Three competing mechanisms explain the recovery pattern.
Each mechanism includes a measurable prediction and a falsifying result.
A priority experiment compares pathway activation with recovery timing.

output_examples.input: Question: why does a material become brittle after repeated heating cycles?
output: The report separates microstructural, chemical, and stress-history hypotheses.
It proposes microscopy, composition analysis, and controlled thermal cycling tests.
A comparison table ranks hypotheses by evidence, feasibility, and explanatory power.

output_examples.input: Goal: prepare a hypothesis brief for a grant planning meeting.
output: The skill creates an executive summary with 3-5 hypotheses.
Appendix plans cover literature review, detailed experiments, and quality assessment.
Figure suggestions include a mechanism diagram and experimental flowchart.

best_practices: Provide the core observation, domain, constraints, and any preliminary data.
Ask for competing hypotheses before choosing a preferred explanation.
Review generated experiments with a qualified domain expert before execution.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to justify a fixed conclusion without testing alternatives.
Do not treat citations or mechanisms as verified without source review.
Do not skip controls, confounds, or falsification criteria in experiment plans.

faq.question: Does this skill run experiments?
faq.answer: No. It designs hypothesis tests and report structures, but experiments require real-world execution.

faq.question: Can it work outside biomedical research?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports scientific domains including materials, physics, ecology, and computational research.

faq.question: Does it require web access?
faq.answer: Not always. Web access helps when current literature, PubMed searches, or recent papers are needed.

faq.question: What output format does it emphasize?
faq.answer: It emphasizes structured hypothesis reports, including a concise main text and detailed appendices.

faq.question: Can it create figures?
faq.answer: It recommends schematic figures and can coordinate with a separate scientific-schematics skill when available.

faq.question: Is the generated science guaranteed correct?
faq.answer: No. Outputs are research drafts that need citation checks, expert review, and empirical testing.
